---
title: "drisk"
---

`drisk` is a Python package designed for quick Monte Carlo modelling, with an emphasis
on distribution elicitation and composable models.

The intention is to make Monte Carlo models ergonomic enough for "back of the envelope"
problems in quick scripts or notebooks, while also supporting more advanced methods for
heavier simulation workloads.

`drisk` is in development, and currently supports univariate continuous and discrete
distribution sampling, correlated sampling using Copulas, composable Monte Carlo
models, and decision support workflows such as decision-tree rollback analysis.

This documentation site collects runnable examples and report-style walkthroughs.

## Quick start

```{python}
import drisk as dr
```

Create a positive, right-skewed LogNormal distribution and plot it.

```{python}
volume = dr.LogNormal.elicit(
    lower=800,
    upper=1_500,
    confidence=0.8,
    name="Monthly sales volume",
)

volume.plot(color="steelblue")
```

Create a PERT distribution from a minimum, most-likely, and maximum estimate.

```{python}
margin = dr.PERT.elicit(
    min=20,
    mode=35,
    max=60,
    name="Unit margin",
)

margin.plot(color="darkorange")
```

Compose the distributions with ordinary Python arithmetic, then simulate the result.

```{python}
monthly_profit = volume * margin
monthly_profit.name = "Simulated Monthly Profit"
samples = monthly_profit.sample(size=50_000, seed=42)
samples[:5]
```

```{python}
monthly_profit.plot(color="seagreen")
```
